name: eGain Corporation
shortDescription: eGain (NASDAQ: EGAN) sells an AI Knowledge Hub - a governed enterprise knowledge-management platform that grounds AI agents and human reps in trusted, compliant answers. FY ends June 30. Q3 FY2026: revenue $22.5M (+7% YoY), AI Knowledge Hub ARR $48.0M (+26% YoY, 64% of SaaS ARR), adj. EBITDA margin ~14%, $80.5M cash, zero debt. Trades ~1.4x EV/Rev on the 2026-06-03 close.
businessModel: Cloud SaaS subscriptions (~92% of revenue) sold direct to large enterprises, with the balance from legacy license/support and professional services. Over 87% of FY2025 recurring cloud revenue came from organizations with >$1B revenue or government entities. Go-to-market combines a direct enterprise sales force with ecosystem partnerships (Cisco/Webex, Amazon Connect/AWS Marketplace, RingCentral, CRM/CCaaS platforms) - one partner is also its single largest customer (~16% of FY2025 revenue). Land-and-expand on a per-seat AI Agent / Knowledge Hub basis. The company is deliberately sunsetting ~$4.7M of legacy messaging ARR (to zero by FY2027) to concentrate on AI Knowledge.
marketContext: eGain competes in the customer-engagement, knowledge-management, and contact-center-AI markets, positioning itself as the governed knowledge layer ('the instruction layer for AI') that solves the 'garbage in, garbage out' problem for enterprise AI deployments and agent assist. It differentiates on AI-driven, compliant, content-lifecycle-governed knowledge for regulated large enterprises (BFSI, healthcare, public sector) versus much larger CX/CCaaS suites. Gartner recognizes eGain as a leader in Generative AI Knowledge Apps. The company is small ($207M market cap) but net-cash, profitable, and re-accelerating on the AI Knowledge mix shift.
customers: 87%+ of FY2025 recurring cloud revenue from large enterprises (>$1B revenue or government)
Achmea (one of Europe's largest cooperative insurers) - ~21,000 users (8,225 contact-center + 12,750 enterprise), each with an AI Agent license (Dec 2025)
Navy Federal Credit Union, Barclays, Vodafone, Virgin Media, Bell Aliant cited among customers
Public sector incl. State of Washington; utilities incl. Southern Water, Yorkshire Water, Scottish & Southern Energy
lastKnownValuation: $207M market cap (~$8/share, 2026-06-03 close); EV ~$129M, $80.5M cash, zero debt; ~1.4x EV/Revenue LTM, ~17.6x forward P/E; 52-wk $5.45-$15.95.
ipoReadiness: Public since 2000 (NASDAQ: EGAN). Net-cash balance sheet ($80.5M cash, no debt); ~27% YTD operating-cash-flow margin. Capital returned via buyback (authorization expanded to $60M aggregate in Sept 2025; ~$20M remaining). 3 covering analysts, mean target $12.83 (range $8-$20) - a thinly covered, founder-led micro-cap.

leadership.name: Ashutosh (Ashu) Roy
leadership.title: Chairman and Chief Executive Officer
leadership.background: Co-founder; has led eGain since 1997. Previously founded WhoWhere? Inc and Parsec Technologies. BS (IIT New Delhi), MS (Johns Hopkins), MBA (Stanford).

leadership.name: Eric Smit
leadership.title: Chief Financial Officer
leadership.background: Over 15 years of finance and operations experience; previously Director of Finance at WhoWhere? Inc.

leadership.name: JC Rao
leadership.title: Senior Vice President, Products and Services
leadership.background: Joined eGain in 1999; progressed through product engineering, management, and customer success roles.

leadership.name: Vishal Nehru
leadership.title: Senior Vice President, Worldwide Sales and Customer Success
leadership.background: Two decades of leadership in strategic sales, customer success, and professional services.

brief.businessOverview: eGain Corporation (NASDAQ: EGAN) is a Sunnyvale, California enterprise SaaS company (fiscal year ending June 30) that sells an AI-powered knowledge-management platform for customer engagement and contact centers. Its flagship AI Knowledge Hub centralizes fragmented enterprise content and delivers trusted, governed, real-time answers to both AI agents and human contact-center reps across voice, chat, email, and self-service. Founder Ashu Roy has run the company since 1997 and is repositioning it around generative-AI knowledge - 'trusted knowledge infrastructure for AI' - while deliberately sunsetting legacy messaging. In Q3 FY2026 (ended March 31, 2026) revenue was $22.5M (+7% YoY) with AI Knowledge Hub ARR of $48.0M (+26% YoY, 64% of total SaaS ARR), adjusted EBITDA margin of ~14%, $80.5M cash, and zero debt. At a ~$207M market cap and ~1.4x EV/Revenue, EGAN is a profitable, net-cash micro-cap whose central debate is whether the AI Knowledge mix shift can re-accelerate the whole company past its high-single-digit headline growth.
brief.productModel: The core product is the eGain AI Knowledge Hub, which connects disparate content sources (SharePoint, CRM knowledge bases, product docs, policies, intranets, legacy systems) and delivers consistent, accurate, governed answers across channels - powering customer-facing AI/virtual agents and agent-assist for human reps. It is powered by AssistGPT, a generative-AI capability for content creation, curation, and authoring, layered with content-lifecycle governance (create, review, approve, translate, retire; duplicate/conflict/broken-link checks). Recent launches extend the platform into the agentic layer: AI Agent IVA (conversational virtual agents), Agentic Studio (multi-agent orchestration via MCP/A2A), an AI Knowledge Suite for retail banking, and connectors that feed governed knowledge to Microsoft Copilot, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini and Cursor - an 'arms-dealer' move positioning the Hub as a system of record for compliant content across the AI ecosystem. Revenue is ~92% recurring SaaS; AI Knowledge Hub ARR ($48.0M, +26% YoY) is the growth engine and now 64% of total SaaS ARR.

recentNews.headline: eGain Reports Q3 FY2026: Revenue +7% to $22.5M, GAAP Net Income $2.4M, AI Knowledge Hub ARR +26%
recentNews.whyItMatters: Profitability inflected sharply (adj. EBITDA margin 14% vs 6% y/y; GAAP EPS $0.09 vs ~$0.00) as the AI Knowledge Hub - ARR up 26% to $48.0M, now 64% of SaaS ARR - becomes the growth engine. Management flagged RFP activity roughly doubling, mostly U.S. Fortune 1000 banking, insurance and healthcare.

recentNews.headline: eGain Launches Agentic Studio with Multi-Agent Orchestration
recentNews.whyItMatters: Agentic Studio coordinates multiple AI agents via MCP and A2A protocols to autonomously resolve complex requests, with actions grounded in policy-compliant knowledge - moving eGain up-stack from knowledge retrieval into the agentic orchestration layer.

recentNews.headline: eGain Announces Enterprise AI Connectors for Microsoft Copilot, Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, and Cursor
recentNews.whyItMatters: Repositions eGain as the governed knowledge layer feeding third-party AI platforms rather than a standalone CX tool - expanding the TAM from contact centers to enterprise-wide AI grounding.

recentNews.headline: eGain Launches AI Knowledge Suite for Retail Banking
recentNews.whyItMatters: A vertical bundle (guided needs-based sales, AscentAI compliance automation, HERE enterprise browser) for community/regional banks and credit unions - directly aligned with the BFSI demand surge management cited on the Q3 call.

recentNews.headline: Achmea Selects eGain AI Knowledge Hub and AI Agent to Power Digital Transformation
recentNews.whyItMatters: Marquee win - one of Europe's largest cooperative insurers - deploying eGain across ~21,000 users, each with an AI Agent license, integrating 26,000+ documents. A large per-seat expansion validating land-and-expand in regulated European BFSI.

recentNews.headline: eGain Expands Stock Repurchase Program to $60M Aggregate with FY2025 Results
recentNews.whyItMatters: Board lifted the buyback authorization from $40M to $60M after repurchasing ~$39.8M - a capital-return signal underscoring management confidence as the AI transition plays out on a net-cash balance sheet.

earningsTakeaways.aiCommentary: AI demand was the dominant theme. AI Knowledge Hub ARR grew 26% YoY to $48.0M and now represents 64% of total SaaS ARR, and management said the mix will keep shifting toward it. CEO Ashu Roy framed knowledge as the foundation for enterprise AI - 'Knowledge is the instruction layer for AI' - arguing that without a clean knowledge foundation, AI deployments degrade into a 'garbage in, garbage out' problem. CFO Eric Smit said AI Knowledge is now being evaluated as enterprise infrastructure rather than solely a contact-center solution, broadening the buying center and deal size. At eGain Solve London the company launched four new platform products, including an AI Knowledge Suite for retail banking, an AI Agent for Cisco Webex Contact Center, and connectors for Microsoft Teams, Slack and Zoom Team Chat.
earningsTakeaways.demandCommentary: Pipeline and RFP activity accelerated sharply: management said trailing-60-day RFP volume was roughly double its average rate, concentrated in U.S. Fortune 1000 BFSI (banking, insurance) and healthcare, attributed to growing awareness of the knowledge-quality problem in AI deployments. Offsetting the strength, the quarter absorbed a one-time ~$1.6M total SaaS ARR reduction (incl. ~$0.9M AI Knowledge) when an EMEA on-premise customer declined to migrate to eGain Cloud and terminated, tied to country-level cloud restrictions; excluding non-core legacy messaging, all-customer SaaS ARR would have grown 11% YoY versus the reported 7%. The company ended with $80.5M cash, no debt, ~27% YTD operating-cash-flow margin, and ~$20M remaining under its buyback authorization. Management guided AI Knowledge ARR to double-digit growth into FY2027.

sources.name: SEC EDGAR - eGain Form 8-K (Q3 FY2026 earnings, filed 2026-05-14)
eGain press release - Reports Third Quarter 2026 Results

keyRisks: Headline growth still high-single-digit: total revenue grew only 7% YoY in Q3 FY2026 and FY2026 revenue is guided to $90.5-91M - roughly flat versus FY2024's $93.1M. The bull case rests entirely on AI Knowledge Hub ARR (+26%) eventually dominating the mix enough to lift the whole company; until then, the consolidated growth rate looks pedestrian for an 'AI' name.

Customer concentration: one customer that is also a channel partner accounted for ~16% of FY2025 revenue (18% in FY2024). Loss or renegotiation of that relationship would be a material revenue and ARR shock.

Legacy drag and lumpy ARR: the deliberate sunset of ~$4.7M legacy messaging ARR (to zero by FY2027) suppresses reported growth, and Q3 showed how a single EMEA customer termination can knock ~$1.6M off SaaS ARR in a quarter - small absolute numbers swing a ~$75M ARR base.

Competitive intensity from scaled CX suites: NICE, Verint, Pegasystems, Salesforce Service Cloud, ServiceNow and Microsoft all embed knowledge/agent-assist into far larger platforms and can bundle it at near-zero incremental price, pressuring a point-solution vendor's pricing and attach.

Micro-cap liquidity and coverage: ~$207M market cap, only 3 covering analysts, ~230k average daily share volume and a 52-week range of $5.45-$15.95 - the stock is thinly traded and volatile, and a founder-chairman-CEO controls the agenda.

AI commoditization risk: if hyperscaler/foundation-model 'knowledge grounding' (RAG, native enterprise connectors) becomes good-enough out of the box, eGain's governed-knowledge differentiation could compress over time even as near-term demand rises.

slideBullets: AI Knowledge Hub is the engine: ARR $48.0M, +26% YoY, now 64% of total SaaS ARR (Q3 FY2026) - the mix shift, not headline 7% revenue growth, is the thesis. Management guides AI Knowledge ARR to double-digit growth into FY2027.

Profitable and net-cash, rare for an 'AI' micro-cap: Q3 adj. EBITDA $3.2M (~14% margin, up from ~6%), GAAP net income $2.4M, $80.5M cash, zero debt, ~27% YTD OCF margin - and ~$20M left on a $60M buyback.

Demand inflecting: RFP volume roughly doubled in the trailing 60 days, concentrated in U.S. Fortune 1000 banking, insurance and healthcare, as enterprises confront the 'garbage in, garbage out' knowledge-quality problem grounding their AI agents.

Platform moving up-stack: new Agentic Studio (multi-agent orchestration via MCP/A2A), AI Agent IVA, a retail-banking suite, and governed-knowledge connectors into Copilot, Claude, Gemini and Cursor expand eGain from contact-center tool to enterprise AI grounding layer.

Cheap and overlooked, but for reasons: ~1.4x EV/Revenue and ~17.6x forward P/E on a $207M cap with only 3 analysts - the discount reflects ~16% single-customer concentration, legacy-messaging drag, lumpy ARR, and competition from far larger CX suites (NICE, Verint, Pega, Salesforce).

businessOverviewSources.name: eGain Q3 FY2026 results press release

keyRisksSources.name: eGain FY2025 Form 10-K (risk factors & customer concentration, SEC EDGAR)

diligenceQuestions: What is the path for total revenue to re-accelerate above high-single-digits? Once legacy messaging fully runs off by FY2027 and AI Knowledge Hub is the dominant ARR component, what consolidated growth rate does management expect, and what net revenue retention is the AI Knowledge base actually compounding at (gross vs. net of the lumpy EMEA-type churn events)?

How durable is the ~16%-of-revenue partner/customer relationship? What are the contract term, renewal timing, and pricing dynamics, and how concentrated is the AI Knowledge Hub ARR within the top 10 logos - i.e., how much of the +26% growth is land-and-expand at a few large accounts (Achmea-style) versus broad new-logo adoption?

Where is the competitive moat against bundling? In head-to-head RFPs against NICE, Verint, Pega, Salesforce Service Cloud and Microsoft, what is eGain's win rate and the basis (governance/compliance, integration breadth, accuracy), and how defensible is the 'governed knowledge layer' as hyperscaler-native RAG and enterprise connectors improve?
